title: pityrocarpa moniliformis
intro: Pityrocarpa moniliformis, also known as the Mexican tea or tila, is a plant native to Mexico that belongs to the hawthorn family (Crataegus). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Pityrocarpa moniliformis include its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Pityrocarpa moniliformis is commonly prepared as a tea by infusing the flowers or leaves in hot water, which can be used to treat various health conditions, and it is also used in herbal supplements and tinctures.
